YaBB.history: Released in 2000 as YaBB (Yet another Bulletin Board); SourceForge lists the project as registered on 2000-07-30. It has remained a volunteer-maintained forum project with no notable acquisition or rebrand history.
YaBB.summary_abuse: YaBB has little standalone abuse signal; it is self-hosted forum software, so abuse depends on the forum operator and usually looks like ordinary spam or scam posts rather than platform-level abuse. It has no central hosting, short-link, or custom-domain SaaS surface to attract scammers.

YachtSys.history: YachtSys is run by S.C. IOmundo S.R.L. in Iași, Romania, and the company has been operating since at least 2011; the product has been marketed under the YachtBooker brand for many years with no notable ownership change or deprecation event visible on the public site.
YachtSys.summary_abuse: YachtSys has no notable abuse signal; it is a niche booking-management SaaS for yacht charter businesses, not a consumer-facing platform that scammers typically target. The public site shows normal business features and support contacts rather than arbitrary-content hosting or high-risk abuse primitives.

Yahoo Advertising.history: Yahoo was founded in 1994 by Stanford students Jerry Yang and David Filo; the advertising business has remained part of the Yahoo brand through later ownership changes, including Apollo’s 2021 acquisition of Yahoo from Verizon Media.
Yahoo Advertising.summary_abuse: Yahoo Ads is a mainstream ad-tech platform, so abuse risk comes from malvertising, scam creatives, and fraudulent traffic more than from abuse-only use. Yahoo publishes transparency and safety materials, and The Paranoids run a public bug-bounty and disclosure path, but the ad-network model still makes it a recurring target for deceptive campaigns.

Yahoo! Ecommerce.history: Launched as Yahoo! Store in the late 1990s for small-business ecommerce. The service later moved under Yahoo Small Business and is now operated as Turbify by Aabaco Small Business, LLC, part of Infinite Computer Solutions.
Yahoo! Ecommerce.summary_abuse: A hosted ecommerce storefront platform, so legitimate use dominates, but it can be abused for fake shops, counterfeit listings, and scam storefronts. Turbify’s terms and site guidelines ban spam, fraud, impersonation, counterfeit or harmful goods, and reserve suspension or termination with abuse checks, which helps curb abuse; there is no free tier, so the classic freemium phishing setup is less of a fit.

Yahoo! Tag Manager.history: Yahoo! Tag Manager is part of Yahoo Japan’s advertising stack, now operated under LY Corporation after the 2023 reorganization of Yahoo Japan and LINE. LY Corporation was founded in 1996 and the service remains active as a hosted tag management tool.
Yahoo! Tag Manager.summary_abuse: Yahoo! Tag Manager has limited abuse signal; it is mainly used legitimately by websites to manage analytics and ad tags. The main risk is account compromise or unauthorized script injection on a site using the container, rather than stand-alone scam use. LY Corporation has a corporate cybersecurity policy, but this product is not known as a common abuse platform.

Yahoo! Web Analytics.history: Launched in 2008 after Yahoo acquired IndexTools and rebranded it as Yahoo! Web Analytics; Yahoo discontinued the service in 2012.
Yahoo! Web Analytics.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal: Yahoo! Web Analytics was a legitimate site-measurement SaaS for marketers and e-commerce sites, not a service commonly targeted by scammers. Its main risk is ordinary analytics tracking on embedded sites, not phishing, malware hosting, or spam infrastructure.

YalinHost.history: Founded in 2021 as YalinHost Internet Hizmetleri by Yunus Emre Özeren in Bursa, Turkey. It has stayed under the same owner and current public presence since launch, with no notable acquisitions or rebrands found.
YalinHost.summary_abuse: Standard hosting and VPS services have some abuse exposure because customers can host arbitrary sites and low-cost infrastructure can be used for phishing or scam landing pages, but there is no strong public signal that YalınHost is abuse-heavy. The visible footprint is mainly legitimate shared hosting, reseller hosting, domains, and VPS, with public support and billing contact details on the site.

Yamada UI.history: Yamada UI launched with v1.0.0 on 2023-12-25 and later released v2.0.0 on 2025-10-17. It is maintained by Hirotomo Yamada and contributors.
Yamada UI.summary_abuse: Yamada UI has no meaningful abuse signal; it is an open-source React component library with no hosted service, account system, or custom-domain surface for scammers to exploit directly. Any abuse would be indirect, through malicious sites built with the library rather than the library itself.

Yampi Checkout.history: Founded in 2011 by Lucas Colette as BUBB, a web development company in Ibitinga, Brazil. Launched its first virtual store in 2014 and its transparent checkout in 2016. Rebranded from BUBB to Yampi in 2019 as a full e-commerce platform. Became remote-first in 2020. No notable ownership changes or acquisitions since founding.
Yampi Checkout.summary_abuse: Yampi Checkout is a Brazilian payment processor with no widely documented abuse profile. As a checkout platform serving over 170,000 merchants, some instances of fraudulent storefronts are possible but do not represent a notable pattern relative to legitimate use. The vendor displays PCI certification and claims robust fraud prevention. Abuse risk is inherent to the payment processing category rather than specific to Yampi.

Yampi Virtual store.history: Founded in 2011 in Ibitinga, Brazil by Lucas Colette as BUBB, initially building corporate websites. Launched its first virtual store platform in 2014. Rebranded to Yampi in 2019 with a full ecommerce and checkout platform. Grew to over 170,000 stores and became remote-first in 2020. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events since.
Yampi Virtual store.summary_abuse: Yampi is a Brazilian ecommerce SaaS platform. Like most hosted storefront platforms, it is occasionally used for fake storefronts and fraudulent product listings, but legitimate SMB use overwhelmingly dominates. The vendor requires KYC (CPF/CNPJ and credit card registration), publishes an AUP prohibiting counterfeit and illegal goods in its terms of use, displays PCI security compliance, and offers a publicly accessible complaints (denuncias) page. No widespread documented abuse campaigns or notable security incidents are associated with the platform.

Yandex SmartCaptcha.history: Yandex SmartCaptcha was launched around 2020 as part of Yandex Cloud, the public cloud platform developed by Russian internet company Yandex. The service evolved from Yandex's earlier captcha technology used across its own properties. In July 2024, Yandex's Russian assets including Yandex Cloud were sold by Dutch parent Nebius Group to a consortium of Russian investors for $5.3 billion amid sanctions related to the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
Yandex SmartCaptcha.summary_abuse: Yandex SmartCaptcha is a security service designed to prevent automated abuse; it does not itself serve as an abuse vector. As a captcha provider, it has no meaningful abuse signal separate from general Yandex privacy concerns around data handling under Russian law.

Yandex.Cloud.history: Launched by Yandex in closed beta April 2018 with general availability later that year. Became an independent Yandex business unit in October 2020. As part of Yandex's broader corporate restructuring, the cloud business was included in the 2024 sale of Yandex's Russian operations to a consortium of investors.
Yandex.Cloud.summary_abuse: Yandex Cloud is a public cloud platform hosting IaaS and PaaS services. Like all major cloud providers, it can be abused for hosting phishing pages, malware C2 infrastructure, and cryptomining, but abuse is a small share of overall use. Yandex publishes an acceptable use policy, provides DDoS protection services, and has standard abuse-reporting channels. No notable public incidents of elevated abuse compared to peer cloud platforms.

Yandex.Cloud CDN.history: Yandex Cloud, including its CDN service, launched in 2018 as Yandex's public cloud platform, entering closed beta in April 2018 and technical preview in September 2018. Yandex itself was founded in 2000 by Arkady Volozh and Ilya Segalovich and grew into Russia's largest technology company. In July 2024, the Dutch holding company Yandex N.V. (renamed Nebius Group) sold the Russian operations including Yandex Cloud to a consortium of Russian investors for $5.3 billion, triggered by international sanctions. Yandex Cloud CDN continues operations under the new ownership structure.
Yandex.Cloud CDN.summary_abuse: Yandex.Cloud CDN is a content delivery network operated by Yandex, serving primarily Russian and CIS markets. As a CDN infrastructure service with usage-based pricing and no free custom-domain tier, its abuse exposure is limited compared to consumer-facing platforms. Yandex publishes an Acceptable Use Policy and DDoS protection is included. The service uses EdgeCenter's infrastructure for CDN nodes and charges per-GB for traffic, creating no economic incentive for abuse hosting. No significant public incidents of abuse have been documented for Yandex Cloud CDN specifically. Yandex operates a bug bounty program and maintains responsible disclosure policies.

Yandex.Direct.history: Yandex.Direct launched in 2001 as the contextual advertising platform for Yandex Search, Russia's dominant search engine. It was one of the first pay-per-click ad systems in the Russian market. In July 2024, following international sanctions over the Ukraine war, the Dutch parent company Nebius Group (formerly Yandex N.V.) sold all Russian assets including Yandex.Direct to a consortium of Russian investors for $5.3 billion. Yandex LLC is now a privately-held Russian company owned by a consortium including Lukoil and senior management.
Yandex.Direct.summary_abuse: Yandex.Direct is an advertising platform with documented abuse patterns typical of large ad networks. A 2022 Bureau of Investigative Journalism report found Yandex ads promoted misinformation about Russia's war in Ukraine. The platform uses extensive user tracking via Yandex.Metrica for ad targeting. As a Russian company, Yandex operates under the Yarovaya law requiring data-sharing with authorities, and has faced FSB demands for encryption keys and user data. Advertisers can target users with relatively detailed demographic and behavioral profiles. Click fraud, misleading offers, and scam landing pages are common abuse vectors in regional ad markets. Anti-abuse enforcement is complicated by Russian government pressure on content moderation. The platform does not offer anonymous signup; advertisers must provide business registration details.

Yandex.Messenger.history: Yandex.Messenger is an instant messaging and live chat application developed by Yandex, the Russian technology company founded in 1997 by Arkady Volozh and Ilya Segalovich. The standalone messenger app was released around 2020, building on earlier Yandex communication and dialog platforms. In July 2024, Yandex's Russian operations were divested from the Dutch holding company Nebius Group and sold to a consortium of Russian investors, making Yandex LLC an independently owned entity.
Yandex.Messenger.summary_abuse: Yandex.Messenger has a low abuse profile. As a live chat and IM platform primarily used in Russia and CIS markets, it is not a preferred tool for global phishing or scam operations. Yandex maintains an acceptable use policy and runs a bug bounty program. The primary abuse vector would be social engineering through embedded live chat widgets, a generic risk common to all such customer communication platforms. No major public incidents of systematic abuse have been documented for this product.

Yandex.Metrika.history: Launched by Yandex in 2008 and made publicly available in 2009 as a free web analytics service. Grew to become the third most widely used web analytics platform globally, with dominant market share in Russian-speaking markets. Introduced innovative features including Webvisor session replay technology. A paid Metrika Pro tier was later added for enterprise-scale users requiring higher data limits and advanced features. Integrates closely with Yandex.Direct advertising platform.
Yandex.Metrika.summary_abuse: Yandex.Metrica is a JavaScript-based web analytics service deployed on millions of websites to collect visitor traffic and behavior data. As with all analytics platforms, it processes significant amounts of visitor data, raising privacy considerations around tracking and data handling. Yandex provides user opt-out mechanisms and data processing controls. No notable incidents of Yandex.Metrica being specifically exploited for large-scale abuse have been documented, though the broader web analytics category faces ongoing privacy scrutiny regarding cross-site tracking and data retention.

Yapla.history: Founded in 2013 in France to provide all-in-one management, fundraising, and payment software for non-profit organisations. The company has expanded to serve Canada, Italy, and Belgium. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, or deprecation events since founding.
Yapla.summary_abuse: Yapla is a non-profit management and payment platform with minimal abuse signal. It operates primarily in regulated EU markets, processes payments for verified associations, and offers a free tier with website building capabilities. The association-focused target market and French regulatory oversight reduce abuse surface. No documented patterns of phishing or scam abuse found in public security research.

Yaws.history: Created by Claes (klacke) Wikstrom around 2002 as a high-concurrency web server written in Erlang. Gained attention from a 2002 benchmark handling 80,000 concurrent connections versus Apache's 4,000 at the SICS lab. Maintained by the Erlang community under the erlyaws GitHub organization; lead maintainer transitioned to Steve Vinoski. No major forks or ownership changes.
Yaws.summary_abuse: Yaws is a self-hosted open-source web server with no notable abuse signal. It has no consumer-facing surface, no free-tier hosting platform, and no anonymous signup flow that fraudsters could exploit. As a backend infrastructure component deployed by operators on their own servers, abuse risk is determined by how it is configured and operated, not by the software itself.

Yay! Forms.history: Founded around 2022 as a US-based AI-powered form builder and Typeform/Jotform alternative. Initially targeted Brazilian and LATAM markets with Brazilian Portuguese and Spanish admin interfaces added November 2023. Small independent startup with no disclosed funding.
Yay! Forms.summary_abuse: Yay! Forms is a form builder SaaS with features that carry inherent abuse risk: custom subdomain at Plus tier and custom domain at Professional tier, a 7-day free trial without credit card requirement, and Facebook Pixel/GA4/GTM tracking built into higher plans — characteristics that can be attractive for phishing page hosting. The vendor added automatic bot protection in October 2024. No notable public incidents of widespread abuse have been documented, and the platform remains relatively small (44,000 users).

Ybug.history: Founded around 2018 as Ybug s.r.o., a Czech visual feedback and bug tracking SaaS. Grew to over 1,000 teams with integrations into Jira, Slack, GitHub, ClickUp, and Asana. Received G2 High Performer awards. No notable ownership changes or acquisitions.
Ybug.summary_abuse: Ybug is a visual feedback widget embedded on client websites for bug tracking. Its annotated screenshot capture could theoretically be misused, but no documented abuse cases exist. The vendor is EU-based, GDPR-compliant, and offers EU-hosted infrastructure. The tool requires deliberate website integration and does not expose anonymous content hosting, URL shortening, or mass-communication features that typically attract abuse.

Yclas.history: Yclas, originally called Open Classifieds, was created in 2009 as a free open-source PHP classifieds script. It later rebranded to Yclas and expanded to offer both a self-hosted open-source version and a cloud-hosted SaaS offering.
Yclas.summary_abuse: Yclas is a classifieds platform that allows users to post ads; classified ad sites can be used for scam listings (fake items for sale, rental fraud), though abuse is proportionate to any marketplace platform. The open-source self-hosted nature means abuse depends heavily on how individual site operators moderate their communities.

Ycode.history: Ycode launched around 2020 as a no-code website and web app builder combining visual frontend design with backend CMS features, positioning itself as a Webflow alternative with workflow builder capabilities.
Ycode.summary_abuse: Ycode is a no-code website builder with no notable abuse signal. It does offer hosted websites under custom domains, but legitimate use strongly dominates and no significant phishing or scam hosting incidents are documented.

Yektanet.history: Founded in 2016, Yektanet grew to become the largest native advertising network in Iran, reaching over 25 million unique users monthly and partnering with 500+ major Persian-language media outlets.
Yektanet.summary_abuse: Yektanet is an Iranian ad network operating primarily within Iran's internet ecosystem. As an ad-tech platform, it collects behavioral data for targeting and ships tracking pixels. No specific international abuse reports are documented, but the platform operates outside GDPR jurisdiction and has limited transparency.

Yell eCommerce.history: Yell is the UK-based business directory brand historically known as Yellow Pages. Yell eCommerce is a managed website and online store product for UK small businesses, now built on Wix technology via a 2023 partnership.
Yell eCommerce.summary_abuse: Yell eCommerce has no notable abuse signal; it is a managed website service for UK small businesses with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would specifically target.

yellow.ai.history: yellow.ai was founded in 2016 in Bangalore, India by Raghu Ravinutala and Jaya Kishore Reddy. Originally focused on chatbot automation, it expanded into a full conversational AI platform for enterprise customer service across messaging channels. The company raised approximately $102 million in funding including a $78.15 million Series C in 2021 and serves major enterprise clients globally.
yellow.ai.summary_abuse: yellow.ai is an enterprise conversational AI and chatbot platform with no notable abuse signal. It is sold to enterprise clients for customer service automation and is not a consumer-facing platform that would attract phishing or scam activity.

Yelp Reservations.history: Yelp Reservations, now marketed as Yelp Guest Manager, is a restaurant table management and online reservations product offered by Yelp Inc. (founded 2004, NYSE: YELP). The product evolved from basic reservation widgets to a full guest management system competing with OpenTable and Resy.
Yelp Reservations.summary_abuse: Yelp Reservations has no notable abuse signal; it is a restaurant management SaaS with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters typically target.

Yelp Review Badge.history: Yelp Review Badge is a free embeddable widget provided by Yelp Inc. (founded 2004, NYSE: YELP) that allows businesses to display their Yelp star ratings and review counts on their own websites.
Yelp Review Badge.summary_abuse: Yelp Review Badge is an embeddable display widget with no notable abuse signal. The widget only shows review data from Yelp's platform and does not expose user-hosted content or anonymous signup paths that would attract fraudsters.

Yepcomm.history: Yepcomm is an e-commerce platform; minimal public information is available about its history or ownership.
Yepcomm.summary_abuse: Yepcomm has no notable abuse signal based on available information.

Yeps.history: Yeps is an email opt-in bar tool for collecting email subscribers via a minimal website banner; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Yeps.summary_abuse: Yeps is an email collection bar widget. Email collection tools can be used to harvest addresses for spam, but Yeps has no specific documented abuse incidents.

YesWiki.history: YesWiki is a French open-source wiki software created around 2012 as a fork of WakkaWiki, aimed at community collaboration and participatory tools for associations and collectives.
YesWiki.summary_abuse: YesWiki is a self-hosted open-source wiki with no notable abuse signal; its use case is community collaboration with no consumer-facing fraud surface.

Yett.history: Yett is a small open-source JavaScript library created by Julien Blatecky (elbywan on GitHub) to allow developers to control the execution of third-party scripts, enabling GDPR-compliant consent-before-analytics workflows. No notable ownership changes or forks.
Yett.summary_abuse: Yett has no abuse signal. It is a small OSS library used to block script execution until user consent is given, with no consumer-facing surface.

Yever.history: Yever is a Brazilian fintech startup based in Belo Horizonte that offers an integrated payment gateway and intelligent checkout platform for small and medium-sized e-commerce businesses in Brazil. The company had processed over R$400 million in transactions by 2024.
Yever.summary_abuse: Yever is a payments and checkout platform serving Brazilian e-commerce merchants with standard KYC and merchant verification requirements. Payment processors carry inherent fraud risk from fraudulent merchants, but Yever's focus on Brazilian SMEs and its anti-fraud features indicate a legitimate posture. No documented abuse-specific incidents.

Yext.history: Founded in 2006 by Howard Lerman, Brian Distelburger, and Brent Metz, Yext began as a local advertising platform and pivoted through several business models before settling on digital knowledge management. The company went public on NYSE under ticker YEXT in 2017.
Yext.summary_abuse: Yext has no notable abuse signal; it is an enterprise digital presence management platform used by multi-location brands and has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters typically target.

Yieldify.history: Founded in 2013 in London, Yieldify is a customer journey optimization and personalization platform. The company was acquired by Epsilon, a data and marketing company owned by Publicis Groupe.
Yieldify.summary_abuse: Yieldify is an on-site personalization platform that embeds tracking pixels and behavioral analytics; it collects user journey data for personalization purposes. No specific phishing or fraud abuse incidents are documented, though it is ad-tech adjacent and ships heavy tracking.

Yieldlab.history: Yieldlab is a German supply-side platform (SSP) and programmatic advertising technology provider, founded around 2008 and based in Hamburg. It is part of the Virtual Minds group, which operates as a European adtech stack provider.
Yieldlab.summary_abuse: Yieldlab is an enterprise programmatic advertising SSP serving European premium publishers. Like all SSPs, it serves ad inventory that could occasionally include malvertising, but as a premium-publisher-focused platform it has stronger vetting than open exchanges. No major abuse incidents are documented.

Yii.history: Yii was created by Qiang Xue and first released in alpha in October 2006, with version 1.0 in December 2008. Yii 2.0 was a ground-up rewrite released in October 2014. Yii 1.1 reached end of life but still receives security patches.
Yii.summary_abuse: Yii has no notable abuse signal; it is a server-side PHP framework with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

YM Cart.history: Founded in 2007; it has operated under 厦门中恒天下网络科技有限公司 without any notable ownership change or deprecation event found.
YM Cart.summary_abuse: YM Cart is a legitimate ecommerce storefront SaaS, but like similar hosted store builders it can be abused for fake-shop scams or phishing-style storefronts. The public site highlights templates, migration tools, and a free-trial signup; I did not find a prominently published abuse-reporting or security program on the marketing site.

YMQ Product Options Variant Option.history: YMQ Product Options Variant Option is a Shopify app developed by YMQ, a small development shop based in Nanjing, China. Launched on the Shopify App Store on March 23, 2021, the app provides unlimited product options and variants functionality beyond Shopify's native limits. The app has gained 348 reviews with a 5.0 rating and is actively maintained.
YMQ Product Options Variant Option.summary_abuse: YMQ Product Options has no notable abuse signal. It is a Shopify app that adds product customization features to merchant stores and does not host user-generated content, offer custom domains, or provide consumer-facing hosting. Abuse risk is limited to the Shopify platform's existing merchant vetting and payment systems.

YNAP Ecommerce.history: Formed in 2015 as YOOX Net-a-Porter Group from the merger of YOOX and Net-a-Porter; in May 2025 the listed parent was renamed LuxExperience B.V.
YNAP Ecommerce.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal; legitimate luxury ecommerce use dominates, and there is no free-tier or anonymous hosting surface for scam content. Misuse would mainly be brand impersonation or phishing aimed at the retailer, not platform-level abuse like user-hosted spam, short links, or arbitrary file hosting.

Yo!Coach.history: Yo!Coach is a tutoring and consultation platform designed to connect learners with experts for one-on-one sessions. It is a small independent SaaS in the online education and marketplace space with limited public history available.
Yo!Coach.summary_abuse: Yo!Coach has no notable abuse signal. It is a marketplace platform connecting learners with tutors, operating in a supervised environment where session participants are known. No documented fraud or phishing patterns are associated with this platform.

YOAPress.history: The yoapress.com domain was registered in 2013. Public pages show the brand Your Online Agents, and the privacy policy was last modified in 2018; no notable acquisitions or deprecation events surfaced.
YOAPress.summary_abuse: YOAPress has no notable abuse signal beyond the generic risk of any managed website builder being used for scam landing pages or deceptive lead-gen sites. The public site is a niche, real-estate-focused service with no visible free tier or obvious anonymous-signup funnel, and I found no public reports of sustained abuse or unusually slow takedowns.

Yoast Duplicate Post.history: Yoast Duplicate Post was originally created as an independent WordPress plugin and later brought under the Yoast family of plugins.
Yoast Duplicate Post.summary_abuse: Yoast Duplicate Post is a WordPress utility plugin with no notable abuse signal.

Yoast SEO.history: Yoast SEO was created by Joost de Valk, with the first plugin released in 2007 under the name WordPress SEO, rebranded to Yoast SEO in 2012. Yoast BV was acquired by Newfold Digital (owner of Bluehost) in August 2021 while continuing to operate independently.
Yoast SEO.summary_abuse: Yoast SEO is a backend WordPress plugin with no notable abuse signal; it processes SEO metadata and has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Yoast SEO for Shopify.history: Yoast SEO for Shopify was launched after Yoast's acquisition by Newfold Digital in 2021, extending the Yoast SEO brand to the Shopify platform as a native Shopify app.
Yoast SEO for Shopify.summary_abuse: Yoast SEO for Shopify has no notable abuse signal; it is an SEO optimization Shopify app with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Yoast SEO Premium.history: Yoast SEO Premium is the paid tier of the Yoast SEO WordPress plugin, sold as an annual subscription per site. Yoast BV was acquired by Newfold Digital in August 2021.
Yoast SEO Premium.summary_abuse: Yoast SEO Premium has no notable abuse signal; it is a WordPress SEO plugin with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Yodel.history: Yodel is a UK parcel delivery and logistics company. Its web platform provides tracking and delivery management for B2B and B2C customers.
Yodel.summary_abuse: Yodel has no notable software-level abuse signal. It is a delivery platform used for shipment tracking; parcel delivery brands are sometimes impersonated in phishing but that relates to brand impersonation, not the platform itself.

YogaTrail.history: YogaTrail is a business management software platform for yoga studios; no notable ownership events documented.
YogaTrail.summary_abuse: YogaTrail is a niche studio management platform with no notable abuse signal.

Yola.history: Yola is a website builder founded in 2007 as Synthasite, rebranded to Yola in 2009, headquartered in San Francisco. It targets small businesses needing simple website creation.
Yola.summary_abuse: Yola is a free-tier website builder that allows users to create websites under custom domains. Free hosting platforms of this type can occasionally be used to host phishing or scam landing pages, though Yola is a minor player with no prominent documented incidents.

Yomdel.history: Yomdel is a UK-based fully managed live chat service that provides human-staffed chat agents to handle website conversations and generate qualified leads for businesses. Founded around 2012, it remains an independent SMB-focused service in the UK market.
Yomdel.summary_abuse: Yomdel has no notable abuse signal. It is a managed live chat service where human agents handle conversations on behalf of client businesses, leaving minimal surface for fraud or phishing abuse.

Yonder.history: Yonder is a booking automation platform targeting tourism and activity businesses, offering booking management, customer feedback collection, review generation, and an AI chatbot for FAQs. It is a small independent SaaS based in New Zealand with limited public history.
Yonder.summary_abuse: Yonder has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche booking and review automation tool for tourism businesses with no consumer-facing anonymous content hosting or features commonly targeted for fraud.

YooMoney.history: YooMoney was originally launched in 2002 as Yandex.Money, a partnership between Yandex and PayCash. In 2020, Sberbank acquired 100% of the service and it was rebranded to YooMoney.
YooMoney.summary_abuse: YooMoney is a Russian e-wallet and payment processor owned by Sberbank. Being a Russian payment platform, it is subject to sanctions and is geofenced outside Russia/CIS. Payment processors can be implicated in money laundering or fraud indirectly; no specific documented incidents for YooMoney specifically were found, but its jurisdiction presents elevated risk for international operators.

Yoori.history: Yoori is a multi-vendor PWA e-commerce CMS developed by SpaGreen in Bangladesh. It is sold as a self-hosted e-commerce solution for marketplace-style stores.
Yoori.summary_abuse: Yoori is a self-hosted e-commerce CMS with no notable abuse signal.

Yopify.history: Yopify is an e-commerce app suite for Shopify stores focused on conversion and customer experience; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Yopify.summary_abuse: Yopify is a Shopify app suite with no notable abuse signal. It operates within Shopify's app ecosystem.

YoPlanning.history: YoPlanning is an online booking and activity planning SaaS targeting leisure and tourism businesses. It is a small independent product with limited public history available.
YoPlanning.summary_abuse: YoPlanning has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche booking management tool for activity businesses with no consumer-facing anonymous content hosting.

Yotpo Loyalty & Referrals.history: Yotpo Loyalty & Referrals is a product of Yotpo, a user-generated content and ecommerce marketing platform founded in 2011 in Israel with US headquarters. Yotpo has raised over $400M in VC funding.
Yotpo Loyalty & Referrals.summary_abuse: Yotpo Loyalty & Referrals has no notable abuse signal; it manages customer loyalty programs and referrals for ecommerce brands without significant fraud exposure.

Yotpo Reviews.history: Yotpo Reviews is the flagship product of Yotpo, founded in 2011 in Israel. It became one of the leading user-generated content platforms for ecommerce, with over $400M raised in VC funding.
Yotpo Reviews.summary_abuse: Yotpo Reviews collects and displays customer reviews. Review platforms can occasionally be targeted for fake review injection or scraping, but Yotpo has anti-spam measures and legitimate use strongly dominates.

Yotpo SMSBump.history: SMSBump was founded in 2018 as an independent SMS marketing platform for Shopify, then acquired by Yotpo to become part of their ecommerce marketing suite.
Yotpo SMSBump.summary_abuse: Yotpo SMSBump is an SMS marketing platform; mass SMS platforms carry inherent spam risk if misused. Yotpo has compliance policies requiring merchant consent verification, but SMS marketing tools can be used for spam if controls are insufficient.

Yotpo Subscriptions.history: Yotpo Subscriptions is a Shopify subscription management app developed by Yotpo as part of their ecommerce retention suite.
Yotpo Subscriptions.summary_abuse: Yotpo Subscriptions is a subscription management app for Shopify merchants with no notable abuse signal documented.

Yottaa.history: Yottaa was founded in 2009 in Waltham, Massachusetts as a web and ecommerce performance optimization company. It operates a multi-CDN acceleration platform serving retail and ecommerce brands.
Yottaa.summary_abuse: Yottaa is an ecommerce performance and CDN platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves as infrastructure for retail websites without consumer-facing fraud exposure.

YouCam Makeup.history: YouCam Makeup is a virtual try-on and augmented reality beauty platform developed by Perfect Corp, a Taiwanese company. It provides AI-powered virtual makeup and cosmetics visualization for ecommerce brands.
YouCam Makeup.summary_abuse: YouCam Makeup is a virtual try-on SaaS for beauty brands with no notable abuse signal; it is embedded by retailers for product visualization.

YouCan.history: YouCan is a MENA-focused ecommerce platform designed to help entrepreneurs in the Arab world build online stores.
YouCan.summary_abuse: YouCan is an ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal documented.

YouCan Pay.history: YouCan Pay is the payment gateway product of YouCan, a Moroccan ecommerce platform founded in 2019 by Mohamed El Ghaissani in Rabat, Morocco. YouCan launched its storefront platform in 2020 and rapidly grew to process over $753 million GMV in 2023, serving 100,000 active stores predominantly in Morocco and the MENA region. YouCan Pay was developed to solve the payment gateway gap in Moroccan ecommerce, supporting Moroccan cards, international cards, and CashPlus cash payments.
YouCan Pay.summary_abuse: YouCan Pay is a Moroccan payment gateway with a modest abuse risk. Payment gateways serving markets with emerging KYC enforcement can see some fraudulent merchant registrations, and Morocco's online market has documented cases of counterfeit storefront fraud. No specific security incidents or fraud reports are publicly attributed to YouCan Pay directly, but the relatively low-friction onboarding and focus on the MENA market warrants a mild caution score.

youCMS.history: youCMS is a Danish enterprise CMS developed by a Danish company and designed for large-scale content management. It is a hosted SaaS product targeting organizations in Denmark and the broader Scandinavian market. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events are documented.
youCMS.summary_abuse: youCMS is a niche Danish enterprise CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is a hosted content management system for legitimate enterprise customers and does not expose consumer-facing features that would attract phishing or fraud activity.

Youform.history: Youform is a no-code conversational form builder for collecting leads and surveys, emerging as a Typeform alternative in 2023.
Youform.summary_abuse: Youform is a form builder. Form tools can be misused for phishing (fake credential harvesting forms), though no specific incidents are documented for Youform.

YouLead.history: YouLead is a Polish sales and marketing automation platform helping businesses manage leads and streamline customer engagement through integrated digital tools.
YouLead.summary_abuse: YouLead is a B2B marketing automation platform with no notable abuse signal.

YouPay.history: YouPay is an Australian ecommerce payment innovation that enables gifting and third-party payment on behalf of shoppers.
YouPay.summary_abuse: YouPay is a novelty gifting payment service with no notable fraud abuse signal documented.

YourGPT.history: YourGPT is a post-2022 platform offering customizable GPT-based tools and chatbots for businesses; it is one of many LLM API wrapper products that emerged after ChatGPT's launch.
YourGPT.summary_abuse: YourGPT is an AI chatbot customization platform wrapping LLM APIs. Like similar post-2022 tools, there is theoretical risk of misuse for social engineering, but no specific documented incidents.

YouTrack.history: YouTrack was first released in 2008 by JetBrains, the Czech software company known for IntelliJ IDEA. It is available as both a cloud SaaS and self-hosted server product, free for up to 10 users.
YouTrack.summary_abuse: YouTrack has no notable abuse signal; it is a developer-facing project management tool with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

YouTube.history: YouTube was founded in February 2005 by Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim. Google acquired it for $1.65 billion in October 2006. It became the world's largest video platform, with over 2 billion logged-in users monthly. It has faced ongoing controversies over content moderation and advertiser safety.
YouTube.summary_abuse: YouTube's embedded player and API are used legitimately on millions of websites. As a platform, YouTube has documented problems with scam ads, fake giveaway streams, and brand impersonation channels, but Google has invested heavily in automated moderation. Abuse of the embedding mechanism (iframing scam content) is occasional but vendor response is active.

Youzan.history: Youzan was founded in 2012 in Hangzhou, China as the largest third-party WeChat e-commerce platform, helping merchants set up social commerce stores on WeChat and Kuaishou. Tencent invested in the company in 2019.
Youzan.summary_abuse: Youzan is a major Chinese ecommerce SaaS platform operating within China's regulated internet environment. No specific fraud incidents are documented, though the Chinese e-commerce ecosystem has general concerns around counterfeit goods and social commerce scams that apply to merchant platforms broadly.

YUI.history: Started at Yahoo in 2005 and released publicly in 2006; active development ended in August 2014, with the project left in maintenance-only mode and then effectively abandoned.
YUI.summary_abuse: YUI has no meaningful abuse signal; it is a front-end JavaScript/CSS library with no consumer-facing hosting, messaging, or redirect surface that scammers typically target.

YUI Doc.history: YUI Doc was part of the YUI Library (Yahoo! User Interface Library), an open-source JavaScript library first released by Yahoo in February 2006. YUI Doc is a Node.js documentation generator used at build time. Yahoo ended active development of YUI in August 2014. The last YUI Doc release was v0.10.0 in January 2016.
YUI Doc.summary_abuse: YUI Doc is a build-time documentation generator with no abuse signal. It has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters could exploit.

Yuno.history: Yuno is a Colombian-founded global payment orchestration platform launched around 2021, designed to help enterprise checkout platforms optimize payment acceptance across multiple providers.
Yuno.summary_abuse: Yuno is a payment orchestration platform with no notable abuse signal; it serves as B2B infrastructure and does not have a consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target directly.

YunoHost.history: YunoHost is a French-origin open-source server OS designed to simplify self-hosting for non-technical users, first released around 2012. It allows one-click deployment of apps like Nextcloud, Mastodon, and WordPress on personal servers.
YunoHost.summary_abuse: YunoHost is a self-hosting operating system with no notable abuse signal; it is infrastructure for personal servers and does not have a consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Yupop.history: Yupop is a commission-free online store platform with no monthly subscription; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Yupop.summary_abuse: Yupop is a free online store builder. Free e-commerce platforms with no KYC requirements can be used to create fake storefronts, but no specific documented incidents exist for Yupop.

Yxper.history: Yxper is a real-time business analytics platform for organizational monitoring; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Yxper.summary_abuse: Yxper is a B2B business analytics platform with no notable abuse signal documented.
